title: celtis crenata
intro: Celtis crenata, also known as the netted hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and other parts of Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Celtis crenata include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Celtis crenata is typically prepared as a decoction or an infusion, with the leaves, bark, and roots being used in herbal preparations, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
